one_time_description: Vedder Crossing Plaza Inc. wound up in voluntary dissolution on March 30, 2021; dividends of $10,158,156 distributed to TzFN upon dissolution
economic_notes: Investments in Shxw Kwimel Cha Limited Partnership (Vedder Crossing Plaza), Centre Creek LP (Chilliwack River Valley property), Ch-ihl-kway-uhk Forestry LP, Ts'elxweyeqw Tribe LP, Seven Generations Environmental Services Ltd., Skynest LP, and BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP. Subsequent event: water line break in land administration building on July 3, 2021.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Vedder Crossing Plaza Inc. dissolved on March 30, 2021 with $10,158,156 distributed to TzFN - significant one-time item boosting surplus. ISC funding jumped from $1.34M to $5.70M year-over-year. TzFN uses modified equity method for its investments in various partnerships and corporations. No schedule of remuneration included in the document. Segments are Governance, Facilities and Programs, Lands Management and Taxation, Public Works and Housing, and Capital. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($5,695,300) plus Other government revenues from segment schedule ($486,034) = $6,181,334. CMHC included in Other government in segment disclosure.
